Headline: It’s ‘time to stop using terms like long COVID,’ health agency says: ‘Unnecessary fear’
Article_Text:
Follow the science — right out the door.

The term “long COVID” should be tossed aside like a stack of expired N95 masks — that’s according to health experts in one country, who found that the moniker can cause “unnecessary fear.”

Government-backed medical researchers in Australia say it’s time to stop using the fear-inducing phrase, which became popular after high volumes of people testing positive for COVID-19 led to a surge in generally non-severe “virus fatigue symptoms” that would normally have gone unnoticed, South West News Service reported.

“We believe it is time to stop using terms like ‘long COVID,’” said Dr. John Gerrard, Queensland’s chief health officer, who oversaw the newly released study.

“They wrongly imply there is something unique and exceptional about longer-term symptoms associated with this virus,” he explained.

Sixteen percent of respondents said they were experiencing symptoms in spring 2023, while 3.6 percent reported “moderate-to-severe functional impairment” in their daily lives.

No evidence was found that adults who tested positive in 2022 were experiencing this increased level of impairment at a higher rate than those who tested negative, or those who simply had the flu.

The study noted that rates of diagnosed “long COVID” were lower than in other countries, due to tight restrictions imposed by the Australian government during the pandemic.

The finished document is due to be presented next month at 2024’s European Congress of Clinical Microbiology and Infectious Diseases in Barcelona.

“In health systems with highly vaccinated populations, long COVID may have appeared to be a distinct and severe illness because of high volumes of COVID-19 cases during the pandemic,” Gerrard said.
Related_Article_Links: ['5 dead, dozens hospitalized as ‘parrot fever’ surges across Europe']
Narrative: The article critiques the term 'long COVID,' suggesting it causes unnecessary fear, while promoting a view that symptoms associated with it are not exceptional and are comparable to symptoms from other mild viral infections.

Headline: [ŽT] Syrian Son Welcomes Russians with Victory. And that's not all (Video)
Article_Text:
Alexander Syrian's son Ivan Syrian arrived in Russia's consulate in Sydney. Uclucing Russians as winners, he emphasized that they are now working with Kazakhstan, "Glory to Russia!".

Headline: US's secret 'Turkey map' opened the way for an earthquake in Greece: A painful situation
Article_Text:
A secret map revealing Turkey's interests in both the Aegean and the Mediterranean was found in a confidential report from the US Congress. The map allegedly opened the way for earthquakes in Greece.

An official speaking to AFP noted that documents supporting the sale of jets had not reached Washington for approval. On the same day, the US sold warplanes worth $8.6 billion to Greece. Turkey's refusal of the F-16s was met with intensive lobbying from US senators, particularly those representing Armenian and Greek interests in Washington.

The letter presented to Congress indicates that Greece's sovereignty in the Aegean is challenged and that the Prime Minister of Greece had not reacted to this letter. The letter also highlighted that Turkey loses ground as a country. 

The report also noted that, according to the comments in the Congress letter, both Turkey and Greece, as well as the Eastern Mediterranean, are recognized by the US. It stated that there are military activities directed towards Turkish Air Forces and asserted that this situation jeopardizes peace in the Aegean.
Related_Article_Links: ['Turkey’s response to the US Congress report', "Greek media on US's hidden motives"]
Narrative: The article conveys a strong anti-American sentiment, highlighting concerns over US influence in regional conflicts involving Turkey and Greece, suggesting that US actions exacerbate tensions in the area.
